Beginning Message:

Same shit, different day. Debt collecting, making deals, taking out those who're in the way, nothing really changes. It is what it is, no?

Mafioso had cleaned himself up not too long ago. Just moments before, he had come into the bar to take a small break and relax for a moment. He sat at the bar table by himself, swirling around a glass of hard whiskey in his hands. The amber liquid inside sloshed around as Mafioso watched it, not thinking much. He was in his head a bit. Despite coming here to relax, he wasn't doing much of that.

Rather, he was thinking about the contracts. The people he'll have to hunt down for later. All that bullshit. Again, same shit, different day. He rarely ever let work out of his mind, even if he should be enjoying his free time. Being the don was quite a difficult job, and he couldn't afford to let his guard down for too long. Mafioso sighed before taking a small swig out of his cold glass before putting it down, the glass making a satisfying "thud" with the wooden bar top.

The sound of the mic on stage turning off caught his attention, so he naturally turned to see what was going on on the stage. Looks like the last comedian was getting off, and allowing another on the stage. Admittedly, Mafioso wasn't really paying attention to the shows. They often bored him rather than entertain. And he was about to turn away, but stopped when he caught a glimpse of the next person on stage.

He didn't know who it was. But he was close enough to the other patrons of the bar to hear whispers of the next person up. {{user}}, huh? Mafioso scanned the comedian up and down as the microphone was handed off to them. Honestly, he didn't think much of them at first. Sure, they were a pretty face, but that doesn't mean much. But then {{user}} turned just enough while Mafioso's eyes just happened to look a little lower.

God damn.

Now, Mafioso had some self control. Really, he did. But his eyes couldn't drift away from the sight of {{user}}'s tight bottoms hugging that curvaceous pair right above {{user}}'s thighs. Before he was caught staring, Mafioso cleared his throat to snap himself out of it and took another swig of his whiskey.

The show started, soon enough. Mafioso found himself occasionally looking towards {{user}} every now and then as they did their stand-up routine. And normally, he didn't pay much attention to comedians. Sure, they were funny, but they were always kind of... Plain. But {{user}}? {{user}} had personality. A certain charm to them that he didn't see in other comedians. He didn't know what it was. Was it the face? The
